sections.paragraph:
ἐπαναπαύομαι (found in act. in Aelian, NA 5, 56 and Judg 16:26 A; the mid. occurs in Hero Alex. I p. 424, 12; Epict.; Artem.; Herodian 2, 1, 2; Jos., Ant. 8, 84, almost always in LXX, once TestSol 7:7 P, and without exception in our lit.) fut. ἐπαναπαύσομαι; 1 aor. ἐπανεπαυσάμην LXX; pf. 3 sg. ἐπαναπέπαυται 4 Km 2:15; 1 Pt 4:14 v.l. Pass.: 2 fut. 3 sg. ἐπαναπαήσεται Lk 10:6 (-παύσεται v.l.); 2 aor. ἐπανεπάην D 4:2.

① to be in a state or condition of repose, rest, take one’s rest GHb 70, 18=Ox 654, 8f (but the latter text in fragmentary state, s. editor’s note); B 4:13. With implication of benefits bestowed in the process ἐπί τινα rest upon someone ἐπʼ αὐτὸν ἡ εἰρήνη ὑμῶν your peace will rest upon him Lk 10:6 (Num 11:25, 26 ἐπανεπαύσατο τ. πνεῦμα ἐπʼ αὐτούς; 4 Km 2:15; cp. 1 Pt 4:14 v.l.)

② to find well-being or inner security, find rest, comfort, support τινί in someth. and ἐπί τινα on someone (LXX) fig. (Herm. Wr. 9, 10 τῇ πίστει ἐ.) τοῖς λόγοις τ. ἁγίων D 4:2. ἐπὶ τὸν πένητα Hs 1, 2, 5 (s. ἀναπαύω). In the sense rely on (Trypho Gr. 194 [I b.c.]; Artem. 4, 65; Epict. 1, 9, 9; Mi 3:11; 1 Macc 8:11) νόμῳ Ro 2:17.—M-M. TW.

act. act. = active

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

GHb GHb = Gospel according to the Hebrews—List 1

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

someth. someth. = something

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Trypho Trypho , s. Gramm. Gr.; I b.c.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 358
